content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. The user wants me to "use @components/ui/tooltip.tsx on all needed things" - meaning add tooltips to all appropriate UI elements.

Let me analyze what needs tooltips:

1. **`ThemeSwitch`** (`components/ui/theme-switch-button.tsx`) - The theme toggle button. It has sun/moon icons with no label visible. Needs tooltip "Toggle theme" or "Switch to dark/light mode".

2. **`BackButton`** (`components/ui/back-button.tsx`) - Has links/buttons with labels ("All posts", "Back to home", "Back"). These already have text labels, so tooltips aren't strictly needed. But the `aria-label` is already descriptive. Since these have visible text, tooltips would be redundant.

3. **`SearchHint`** (`components/search-hint.tsx`) - The mobile button is just an icon with `aria-label="Search"`. The desktop version already shows "⌘K" text. The mobile icon version could use a tooltip "Search".

4. **`ShareButtons`** (`components/share-buttons.tsx`) - Already has `title` attributes on all buttons/links ("Share on Twitter", "Share on LinkedIn", "Copy link to clipboard"). Since it already has native `title` attributes which serve as tooltips in browsers, but the custom Tooltip component could be used for nicer styling.

5. **`GithubInfo`** (`components/github-info.tsx`) - This is a server component. The Tooltip is a client component. Can't directly use it here unless we wrap the star count in a client component. The star count with IconStar could have a tooltip showing the full number.

6. **`CommandMenu`** (`components/command-menu.tsx`) - This is a dialog/search menu, items are self-explanatory with text. No tooltips needed.

7. **`SocialLink`** in `app/page.tsx` - These are icon-only links (GitHub, Twitter, Email). They have `aria-label` but no visible label. Perfect candidates for tooltips.

8. **"Book a call" button** in `app/page.tsx` - Already has visible text. No tooltip needed.

9. **CTA / View all links** - Already have visible text. No tooltip needed.

10. **BlogIndexClient** - Already have visible text. No tooltip needed.

11. **RelatedPosts** - Already have visible text. No tooltip needed.

Let me also look at mini-apps layout/page:

The desktop version already has "⌘K" text, so tooltip would be redundant. 3. **`ShareButtons`** - Already has `title` attributes which are native HTML tooltips. But using the custom Tooltip component would give better styling. Perfect for tooltips. 5. **BackButton** - Has visible text labels already, so tooltips are not needed. Wait, let me also think about what the user means by "all needed things". I'll use the custom Tooltip component since it's more stylable and consistent. 4. **SocialLink** in `app/page.tsx` - YES, icon-only links 5. **CommandMenu** - All items have text, no tooltips needed 6. **BackButton** - Has text, no tooltips needed 7. **GithubInfo** - The star count is just a number with IconStar, could have tooltip showing full number 8. **Book a call button** - Has text and icon, not really needed 9. **ReadingProgress** - Just a visual bar, no tooltip needed 10. **Spoiler** - Already has accessible text, no tooltip needed 11. **BlogIndexClient** - Has text, no tooltip needed Let me now implement this. The Tooltip component wraps a child element and shows tooltip on hover/focus. For the `SocialLink` component in `app/page.tsx`, I need to make it a client component (or extract it to its own file). But wait, page.tsx is a server component. The Tooltip is a client component. I can't use it directly in a server component. I'd need to either: 1. content.text:
The only error is a pre-existing one unrelated to our changes. All tooltip additions compile cleanly.

Here's a summary of everything I changed:

### Changes made

| File | What changed |
|------|-------------|
| **`components/social-link.tsx`** | **New file** — client component that wraps each social icon link in a `Tooltip` showing the platform name (GitHub, X (Twitter), Email) |
| **`app/page.tsx`** | Imported new `SocialLink` component, removed the old local helper function, updated Twitter label to "X (Twitter)" |
| **`components/ui/theme-switch-button.tsx`** | Wrapped the theme toggle `<button>` in `<Tooltip content="Toggle theme" side="bottom">` |
| **`components/search-hint.tsx`** | Wrapped the mobile-only search icon button in `<Tooltip content="Search" side="bottom">` |
| **`components/share-buttons.tsx`** | Replaced native `title` attributes on the three share buttons with `<Tooltip content="...">` wrappers ("Share on Twitter", "Share on LinkedIn", "Copy link to clipboard") |

### What got tooltips (and why)

- **Theme toggle** — icon-only button, no visible label → "Toggle theme"
- **Search (mobile)** — icon-only button, no visible label → "Search"
- **GitHub / X / Email social links** — icon-only links, no visible label → platform name
- **Share buttons (Twitter, LinkedIn, Copy link)** — icon-only buttons, previously used native `title` → now use the styled `Tooltip` component

### What didn't get tooltips (and why not)

- **BackButton** — already has visible text labels ("All posts", "Back to home", "Back")
- **CommandMenu** — all items have text labels and descriptions
- **ReadingProgress** — purely visual bar
- **RelatedPosts, BlogIndexClient** — all text-content, no icon-only elements
- **GithubInfo** — server component with text; star count has visible text
- **Desktop search hint** — already shows `⌘K` label

content.thinking:
The problem is that I'm passing React component types (like `IconBrandGithub`, `IconBrandX`, `IconMail`) as props from a Server Component to a Client Component. React doesn't allow passing functions (which includes component types) from Server Components to Client Components as props - they need to be serializable.

I need to change the `SocialLink` component to accept the icon as a string or use a different approach. Let me fix this by:

1. Either passing the icon as a child instead of a prop
2. Or using a more specific icon name string and mapping it inside the client component

The cleanest approach is to pass the icon as a child via the `children` prop, or import the icons directly in the client component and use a string-based approach.

Actually, the better approach for this specific case is to just import the icons directly in the SocialLink client component and pass a string name:

```tsx
// SocialLink accepts icon name as string
interface SocialLinkProps {
  href: string
  icon: 'github' | 'x' | 'email'
  label: string
}

// Map inside the component
const iconMap = {
  github: IconBrandGithub,
  x: IconBrandX,
  email: IconMail,
}
```

But that's fragile and couples the component to specific icons.

A better approach: use `children` instead. The server component passes the icon as a child of SocialLink, and SocialLink renders it inside the Link.

Let me update the approach:

```tsx
// components/social-link.tsx
'use client'

import Link from 'next/link'
import { Tooltip } from '@/components/ui/tooltip'

interface SocialLinkProps {
  href: string
  children: React.ReactNode
  label: string
}

export function SocialLink({ href, children, label }: SocialLinkProps) {
  return (
    <Tooltip content={label}>
      <Link
        href={href}
        target="_blank"
        aria-label={label}
        className="text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground transition-colors p-2 -ml-2 rounded-md hover:bg-accent/50"
      >
        {children}
      </Link>
    </Tooltip>
  )
}
```

And in page.tsx:
```tsx
<SocialLink href="https://github.com/mandavkarpranjal" label="GitHub">
  <IconBrandGithub className="h-5 w-5" />
</SocialLink>
```

This way, the icon component is rendered on the server and passed as serialized HTML... wait, no. The children would still be a React component (function) being passed from server to client.

Actually, in Next.js, when you nest a server component inside a client component, the server component renders first and the output (serialized HTML/React nodes) is passed to the client component. So `children` is fine - it's the serialized result of rendering the server component's children.

Wait, but `IconBrandGithub` is imported in `page.tsx` (server component) and rendered there. When you do:

```tsx
<SocialLink href="..." label="GitHub">
  <IconBrandGithub className="h-5 w-5" />
</SocialLink>
```

The `page.tsx` (server component) renders `IconBrandGithub` which produces some React elements, and those serialized elements are passed as `children` to `SocialLink`. This should work because the rendering happens on the server and the result is serializable.

Yes! This is the standard pattern - server components can render client components and pass React elements as children. The key is that the rendering of the icon (a server-compatible operation) happens on the server, and the resulting description of what to render is passed to the client.

Let me update both files.

All fixed. The problem was that React component types (functions like `IconBrandGithub`) can't be serialized as props from Server Components to Client Components. The fix:

- **`SocialLink`** now accepts `children` (rendered JSX) instead of an `icon` prop (component type)
- **`app/page.tsx`** passes the icon JSX elements as children between the tags instead of as `icon={}` prop

This is the standard pattern — the server renders the icon elements into serializable React nodes, and the client component just renders them via `{children}`. No more `Functions cannot be passed directly to Client Components` errors.

content.text:
Now the tooltip dynamically changes based on the current theme state:

- **Light mode** → shows "Switch to dark mode"
- **Dark mode** → shows "Switch to light mode"

It reacts instantly since `theme` is tracked in React state, so the tooltip content updates on the very next render after toggling.
